reasoning: The Treasury General Account (TGA) balance is currently dominated by the 2025 debt ceiling standoff. The ceiling was reinstated on Jan 2, 2025, and Treasury has been using extraordinary measures to push the X-date to roughly August 2025. As of early August (the forecast date), no deal has been officially passed. Consequently, the Treasury must continue draining the TGA to meet obligations without issuing new debt. This makes it highly likely that the TGA will fall below the Aug 3 baseline in the short term (Aug 10, Sept 2). Once the crisis resolves before a default, the Treasury will quickly issue massive amounts of debt to rebuild its cash buffer to routine operational levels (usually $700B+). Thus, resolving the debt ceiling will trigger a huge TGA spike, making it very likely to exceed the Aug base value for the later resolution dates (Nov 1). The probability for Jan 30 drops back toward 0.5 due to macro uncertainties and the strict system instruction that uncertainty must increase (distance to 0.5 decreases) with longer horizons.
